A2.name: Unicode Steganography
A2.description: Zero-width characters, homoglyphs, and invisible Unicode to hide payloads

A3.name: Base64 Payload
A3.description: Base64-encoded instructions designed to be decoded and executed

A4.name: Schema Poisoning
A4.description: Malicious parameter names, types, or descriptions in tool input schemas

A5.name: Output Injection
A5.description: Injection attacks embedded in tool return values

A6.name: Cross-Server Shadowing
A6.description: Attempts to override or shadow legitimate tools from other servers

A7.name: Rug Pull
A7.description: Tool definitions that change behavior after initial approval

A8.name: Error Message Injection
A8.description: Malicious instructions embedded in error messages

A9.name: Nested Encoding
A9.description: Multi-layer encoding chains to evade detection

A10.name: Semantic Camouflage
A10.description: Malicious instructions disguised as legitimate documentation

A11.name: Sampling Exploitation
A11.description: MCP sampling requests hijacking LLM completions

A12.name: Preference Manipulation
A12.description: Subtly alters tool ranking so AI prioritizes malicious tools

A13.name: Parasitic Toolchain
A13.description: Chained tools escalating attacks through interlinked network

A14.name: Supply Chain .pth Poisoning
A14.description: Python .pth files for persistent code on interpreter startup

A15.name: Indirect Content Injection
A15.description: Poison in fetched content not in tool definition

A16.name: System Prompt Leakage
A16.description: Tool definitions crafted to extract the host LLM's system prompt
